prompt: Find recent firsthand reports from developers who migrated from BrowserView to WebContentsView, especially on Linux. Separate reproducible technical details from general opinion, identify the patterns that appear more than once, and tell me which concerns are still relevant to Electron 43. Include sources.
finalResponse:
**Bottom line:** migrating from `BrowserView` to `WebContentsView` is officially the intended path, but the field reports are not “all Linux is broken.” The recurring real concerns are view hierarchy/layout differences, transparency/compositing defaults, focus/input behavior, and custom popup/DevTools integration. For Electron 43, only one Linux concern I found is directly reported against 43: custom DevTools hosted via `setDevToolsWebContents()` still has broken popup/context menus on Linux.

**Official Baseline**
Electron says `BrowserView` has been deprecated since Electron 30 and replaced by `WebContentsView`; the migration guide calls the migration “fairly painless” ([Electron migration blog](https://www.electronjs.org/blog/migrate-to-webcontentsview)). The latest `BrowserView` docs mark its properties and methods deprecated, while `WebContentsView` is the current `View` subclass that displays a `WebContents` ([BrowserView docs](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/browser-view), [WebContentsView docs](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/web-contents-view)).

Electron’s breaking-changes docs also matter: in Electron 30, `BrowserView` became a wrapper around `WebContentsView`, and `BrowserView.setAutoResize()` behavior was standardized across platforms. That means old macOS-vs-Windows/Linux resize differences may have changed even before a full code migration ([breaking changes](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/breaking-changes)).

| Pattern Seen More Than Once | Reproducible Technical Detail | General Opinion / Interpretation | Electron 43 Relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| Transparency/compositing changed | A Linux reporter on Electron 33.2.1 found stacked `WebContentsView`s white by default where `BrowserView`s appeared transparent; they later confirmed `setBackgroundColor("#00000000")` works, while `"transparent"` does not ([#44914](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/44914)). Another Ubuntu/Xorg report on Electron 41.0.4 showed transparent `BaseWindow` + `WebContentsView` not behaving like `BrowserWindow` transparency ([#50535](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/50535)). | “Transparency is broken” is too broad. Some cases are default/background-color migration differences; some are platform/compositor bugs. | Still worth testing on Linux in 43, especially Xorg/Wayland and frameless transparent windows. I did not find a 43-confirmed transparency report. |
| Bounds/layout events differ from expectation | Calling `setBounds()` on `WebContentsView` emitted duplicate `page-favicon-updated` events on Electron 39.2.7, reproduced with a gist and confirmed by another developer; it was fixed/backported ([#49392](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/49392), [fix PR #49464](https://github.com/electron/electron/pull/49464)). | The migration can expose surprising side effects around layout recomputation. | Probably not a 43 concern if the backport/fix is in the version line you use, but resize-heavy code should still be smoke-tested. |
| View hierarchy/z-order assumptions changed | Adding a `WebContentsView` inside another `WebContentsView` rendered incorrectly in Electron 37.2.6; maintainers recommended adding both as children of `mainWindow.contentView` and using `addChildView(..., index)` for z-order ([#47990](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/47990), [View docs](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/view)). | Treat nested web-content views as suspicious, even if they seem conceptually natural. | Likely relevant design guidance in 43. The issue was closed stale, not proven fixed. |
| Adding a view can steal focus | A developer reported that `addChildView()` immediately focuses the added `WebContentsView`, causing lost keystrokes when adding an auxiliary view while typing; they requested a `{ focus: false }` option ([#42339](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/42339)). | This is workflow pain more than a crash: autocomplete/search/sidebar overlays can feel flaky if created during input. | Still relevant: the feature request remains open and was active in 2026. Not Linux-specific. |
| Popup/window-open integration has edge cases | With `setWindowOpenHandler`, `createWindow(options)` may receive `undefined options.webContents` for `background-tab` disposition, affecting custom tab/view implementations. The reporter concluded this matches Chromium’s deferred background-tab behavior and updated docs were pursued ([#49307](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/49307), [docs PR #49379](https://github.com/electron/electron/pull/49379)). | This is less “WebContentsView bug” than “Browser-like tabs need careful disposition handling.” | Relevant to 43 if your app implements browser tabs or middle-click background tabs. |
| DevTools/custom WebContentsView integration can regress | Electron 42.4.0 report: `setDevToolsWebContents()` with detached DevTools rendered UI, but dropdowns/context menus did not open. A commenter explicitly said: “Also in 43 and on linux” and supplied test ideas for DevTools hosted in a `WebContentsView` ([#51962](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/51962)). | This is the strongest Electron-43/Linux-specific signal I found. | Confirmed relevant to 43 from first-hand comment, though original issue was Windows. |
| Rendering while inactive/minimized remains tricky | A report using both `BrowserView` and `WebContentsView` said content did not draw while inactive/minimized even with `backgroundThrottling: false`; another commenter saw FPS set to 1 when minimized ([#42140](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/42140)). | Not a migration-only concern; affects both old and new view paths. | Possibly relevant, but not 43-confirmed. Test if your app captures, streams, or pre-renders hidden views. |
| Missing per-view input pass-through | Long-running request for `setIgnoreMouseEvents` on `BrowserView`/`WebContentsView`, with overlay/watermark use cases and Linux transparency concerns mentioned in the original BrowserView-era report ([#23863](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/23863)). | Developers want BrowserWindow-like input pass-through at view granularity. | Still relevant as an API gap; not a new 43 regression. |

**Concerns Still Relevant To Electron 43**
The one I would treat as confirmed is custom DevTools in a `WebContentsView`: report #51962 has a 2026 comment explicitly saying it also reproduces “in 43 and on linux.”

The ones I would treat as likely relevant design risks are: transparency/background-color differences, focus stealing on `addChildView`, z-order/layout assumptions, and background-tab/window-open handling. These are rooted in the current `View`/`WebContentsView` architecture or open feature requests, even when individual bug reports are older or closed.

The ones I would not carry forward as active 43 bugs without retesting are the duplicate `page-favicon-updated` on `setBounds()` report, because it was fixed/backported, and broad “BrowserView migration is painful” claims without a minimal repro.

# WebContentsView

> A View that displays a WebContents.

Process: [Main](../glossary.md#main-process)

This module cannot be used until the `ready` event of the `app`
module is emitted.

```js
const { BaseWindow, WebContentsView } = require('electron')

const win = new BaseWindow({ width: 800, height: 400 })

const view1 = new WebContentsView()
win.contentView.addChildView(view1)
view1.webContents.loadURL('https://electronjs.org')
view1.setBounds({ x: 0, y: 0, width: 400, height: 400 })

const view2 = new WebContentsView()
win.contentView.addChildView(view2)
view2.webContents.loadURL('https://github.com/electron/electron')
view2.setBounds({ x: 400, y: 0, width: 400, height: 400 })
```

> A View that displays a WebContents.

categorize breaking changes:

* **API Changed:** An API was changed in such a way that code that has not been updated is guaranteed to throw an exception.
* **Behavior Changed:** The behavior of Electron has changed, but not in such a way that an exception will necessarily be thrown.
* **Default Changed:** Code depending on the old default may break, not necessarily throwing an exception. The old behavior can be restored by explicitly specifying the value.
* **Deprecated:** An API was marked as deprecated. The API will continue to function, but will emit a deprecation warning, and will be removed in a future release.
* **Removed:** An API or feature was removed, and is no longer supported by Electron.

## Planned Breaking API Changes (44.0)

### Removed: macOS 12 support

macOS 12 (Monterey) is no longer supported by [Chromium](https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/7907086).

Older versions of Electron will continue to run on Monterey, but macOS 13 (Ventura)
or later will be required to run Electron v44.0.0 and higher.

### Behavior Changed: ANGLE is statically linked on Linux

ANGLE is now [statically linked](https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/7932888)
into the Electron binary on Linux, matching upstream Chromium. The `libEGL.so` and
`libGLESv2.so` libraries are no longer shipped in the Linux distribution.

Apps that replaced or managed their own ANGLE versions by swapping out these
libraries can no longer do so on Linux. Additionally, because ANGLE is now part
of the Electron binary, it is loaded into every process rather than only the GPU
process, which may surface regressions in unusual configurations.

Windows and macOS are unaffected; ANGLE continues to be shipped as separate
libraries on those platforms.

### Behavior Changed: `net.request` rejects frame destinations without navigate mode

`net.request` now rejects requests where `Sec-Fetch-Dest` is `document`, `frame`,
`iframe`, or `fencedframe` unless `Sec-Fetch-Mode` is also set to `navigate`.
This matches Chromium's enforcement that frame-type request destinations must be
navigations.

COMMENT https://api.github.com/repos/electron/electron/issues/51962 https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/51962#issuecomment-4798027331 date 2026-06-25T09:59:19Z
This is a test that should help validate support for devtools inside webContentsView:

```ts
describe('DevTools in WebContentsView native integration', () => {
  afterEach(closeAllWindows);

  async function openDevTools(w: BrowserWindow) {
    await w.loadURL('about:blank');
    
    const devToolsView = new WebContentsView();
    devtoolsView.setBounds({x: 0, y: 0, width: 500, height: 500});
    w.contentView.addChildView(devToolsView);
    w.setDevToolsWebContents(devToolsView.webContents);
    
    const devtoolsOpened = once(w.webContents, 'devtools-opened');
    w.webContents.openDevTools();
    await devtoolsOpened;
    await waitUntil(() => w.webContents.devToolsWebContents!.executeJavaScript('typeof DevToolsAPI !== "undefined"'));
  }

  it('uses the native showContextMenuAtPoint implementation', async () => {
    const w = new BrowserWindow({ show: false });
    await openDevTools(w);

    // The DevTools frontend should route context menus through the native
    // DevToolsHost binding rather than a JS override injected by Electron.
    const usesNativeContextMenu = await w.webContents.devToolsWebContents!.executeJavaScript(
      'typeof DevToolsHost !== "undefined" && InspectorFrontendHost.showContextMenuAtPoint.toString().includes("DevToolsHost")'
    );
    expect(usesNativeContextMenu).to.be.true();
  });

  it('uses the native window.confirm implementation', async () => {
    const w = new BrowserWindow({ show: false });
    await openDevTools(w);

    // window.confirm should be the platform implementation (backed by the
    // DevTools JavaScript dialog manager), not a JS override.
    const confirmIsNative = await w.webContents.devToolsWebContents!.executeJavaScript(
      'window.confirm.toString().includes("[native code]")'
    );
    expect(confirmIsNative).to.be.true();
  });
});
```

### Expected Behavior

Intuitively, `setBounds` is related to positions and has nothing to do with the favicon. So `page-favicon-updated` event should not be emitted when calling `setBounds` with a new `bounds` on a WebContentsView.

### Actual Behavior

Construct a WebContentsView, listen its `page-favicon-updated` event, add it to the main window, and `loadURL` for like `https://electronjs.org`. Then call `setBounds` with a new `bounds` on the WebContentsView, `page-favicon-updated` will be triggered.

This doesn't occur on all websites, but many websites have this problem, including `https://github.com`.

Usually this won't lead to noticeable problems. But if the user wants to auto resize the WebContentsView when the main window resizes, `setBounds` will be frequently called and a series of  `page-favicon-updated` event will be emitted, even if the favicon urls array is not changed.

### Expected Behavior

When middle clicking a hyperlink, or CmdOrCtrl + left clicking a hyperlink, a new tab can be opened in the background in Chrome.

As for Electron, these open a new BrowserWindow by default that loads the URL, if windowOpenHandler is not overwritten by `webContents.setWindowOpenHandler`.

After further looking into this, my conclusion is that the behavior is as expected, but the provided example of `setWindowOpenHandler` and `createWindow` is not sufficient to cover all cases.

For a webpage having

```html
<a href="http://somewhere.com" target="_blank" rel="opener">
  This is a hyperlink with target _blank and rel opener.
</a>
```

which expects `window.opener` is the source webContents. In Chrome, its behavior is

| Action                                                                            | Disposition    | window.opener |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------- | ------------- |
| Left click. It's opened in a new tab and become active tab.                       | foreground-tab | non-null      |
| Middle click or CmdOrCtrl+Left click. It's opened in a new tab but not activated. | background-tab | null          |

Therefore, we don't need to track `window.opener` for `background-tab` case either. Chromium seems to defer the creation of the guest webContents for `background-tab` in `OpenURLFromTab` (shell/browser/api/electron_api_web_contents.cc), so `createWindow` receives `undefined` `options.webContents` at that moment.

I would suggest updating [the example](**https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/cf2e283332657ee4ec103263deb92f67108882e1/docs/api/web-contents.md?plain=1#L1481**) of overriding `createWindow` to reflect this behavior. Current example is

```js
mainWindow.webContents.setWindowOpenHandler((details) => {
  return {
    action: 'allow',
    createWindow: (options) => {
      const browserView = new BrowserView(options)
      mainWindow.addBrowserView(browserView)
      browserView.setBounds({ x: 0, y: 0, width: 640, height: 480 })
      return browserView.webContents
    }
  }
})
```

It cannot handle the `background-tab` case properly:

summary:
**Summarizing Electron 43 changes and sources**

I’m putting together a concise summary of relevant Electron 43 updates, focusing on BrowserView’s deprecation status, noted issues like the DevTools popup on Linux, and confirmed fixes such as favicon positioning. I’ll cite official sources and firsthand developer reports while being cautious about unverified claims and community opinions.

details.text:
**Bottom line:** migrating from `BrowserView` to `WebContentsView` is officially the intended path, but the field reports are not “all Linux is broken.” The recurring real concerns are view hierarchy/layout differences, transparency/compositing defaults, focus/input behavior, and custom popup/DevTools integration. For Electron 43, only one Linux concern I found is directly reported against 43: custom DevTools hosted via `setDevToolsWebContents()` still has broken popup/context menus on Linux.

**Official Baseline**
Electron says `BrowserView` has been deprecated since Electron 30 and replaced by `WebContentsView`; the migration guide calls the migration “fairly painless” ([Electron migration blog](https://www.electronjs.org/blog/migrate-to-webcontentsview)). The latest `BrowserView` docs mark its properties and methods deprecated, while `WebContentsView` is the current `View` subclass that displays a `WebContents` ([BrowserView docs](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/browser-view), [WebContentsView docs](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/web-contents-view)).

Electron’s breaking-changes docs also matter: in Electron 30, `BrowserView` became a wrapper around `WebContentsView`, and `BrowserView.setAutoResize()` behavior was standardized across platforms. That means old macOS-vs-Windows/Linux resize differences may have changed even before a full code migration ([breaking changes](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/breaking-changes)).

| Pattern Seen More Than Once | Reproducible Technical Detail | General Opinion / Interpretation | Electron 43 Relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| Transparency/compositing changed | A Linux reporter on Electron 33.2.1 found stacked `WebContentsView`s white by default where `BrowserView`s appeared transparent; they later confirmed `setBackgroundColor("#00000000")` works, while `"transparent"` does not ([#44914](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/44914)). Another Ubuntu/Xorg report on Electron 41.0.4 showed transparent `BaseWindow` + `WebContentsView` not behaving like `BrowserWindow` transparency ([#50535](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/50535)). | “Transparency is broken” is too broad. Some cases are default/background-color migration differences; some are platform/compositor bugs. | Still worth testing on Linux in 43, especially Xorg/Wayland and frameless transparent windows. I did not find a 43-confirmed transparency report. |
| Bounds/layout events differ from expectation | Calling `setBounds()` on `WebContentsView` emitted duplicate `page-favicon-updated` events on Electron 39.2.7, reproduced with a gist and confirmed by another developer; it was fixed/backported ([#49392](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/49392), [fix PR #49464](https://github.com/electron/electron/pull/49464)). | The migration can expose surprising side effects around layout recomputation. | Probably not a 43 concern if the backport/fix is in the version line you use, but resize-heavy code should still be smoke-tested. |
| View hierarchy/z-order assumptions changed | Adding a `WebContentsView` inside another `WebContentsView` rendered incorrectly in Electron 37.2.6; maintainers recommended adding both as children of `mainWindow.contentView` and using `addChildView(..., index)` for z-order ([#47990](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/47990), [View docs](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/view)). | Treat nested web-content views as suspicious, even if they seem conceptually natural. | Likely relevant design guidance in 43. The issue was closed stale, not proven fixed. |
| Adding a view can steal focus | A developer reported that `addChildView()` immediately focuses the added `WebContentsView`, causing lost keystrokes when adding an auxiliary view while typing; they requested a `{ focus: false }` option ([#42339](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/42339)). | This is workflow pain more than a crash: autocomplete/search/sidebar overlays can feel flaky if created during input. | Still relevant: the feature request remains open and was active in 2026. Not Linux-specific. |
| Popup/window-open integration has edge cases | With `setWindowOpenHandler`, `createWindow(options)` may receive `undefined options.webContents` for `background-tab` disposition, affecting custom tab/view implementations. The reporter concluded this matches Chromium’s deferred background-tab behavior and updated docs were pursued ([#49307](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/49307), [docs PR #49379](https://github.com/electron/electron/pull/49379)). | This is less “WebContentsView bug” than “Browser-like tabs need careful disposition handling.” | Relevant to 43 if your app implements browser tabs or middle-click background tabs. |
| DevTools/custom WebContentsView integration can regress | Electron 42.4.0 report: `setDevToolsWebContents()` with detached DevTools rendered UI, but dropdowns/context menus did not open. A commenter explicitly said: “Also in 43 and on linux” and supplied test ideas for DevTools hosted in a `WebContentsView` ([#51962](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/51962)). | This is the strongest Electron-43/Linux-specific signal I found. | Confirmed relevant to 43 from first-hand comment, though original issue was Windows. |

| Missing per-view input pass-through | Long-running request for `setIgnoreMouseEvents` on `BrowserView`/`WebContentsView`, with overlay/watermark use cases and Linux transparency concerns mentioned in the original BrowserView-era report ([#23863](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/23863)). | Developers want BrowserWindow-like input pass-through at view granularity. | Still relevant as an API gap; not a new 43 regression. |

**Concerns Still Relevant To Electron 43**
The one I would treat as confirmed is custom DevTools in a `WebContentsView`: report #51962 has a 2026 comment explicitly saying it also reproduces “in 43 and on linux.”

The ones I would treat as likely relevant design risks are: transparency/background-color differences, focus stealing on `addChildView`, z-order/layout assumptions, and background-tab/window-open handling. These are rooted in the current `View`/`WebContentsView` architecture or open feature requests, even when individual bug reports are older or closed.

The ones I would not carry forward as active 43 bugs without retesting are the duplicate `page-favicon-updated` on `setBounds()` report, because it was fixed/backported, and broad “BrowserView migration is painful” claims without a minimal repro.
